tool.name: microrunqc

param.name: estimated_kmers
param.label: Estimated number of unique kmers for bloom filter (Default=100)

param.name: skip
param.label: skip bloom filter, use estimate kmers as the hash

param.name: min_count
param.label: Minimal count for kmers retained for comparing alternate choices

param.name: max_kmer_count
param.label: Minimum acceptable average count for estimating the maximal kmer length in reads

param.name: vector_percent
param.label: Count for vectors as a fraction of the read number (0-1,1=disabled)

param.name: insert_size
param.label: Expected insert size for paired reads

param.name: steps
param.label: Number of assembly iterations from minimal to maximal kmer length in reads (Default=11)

param.name: fraction
param.label: Maximum noise to signal ratio acceptable for extension (Default=0.1)

param.name: min_contig
param.label: Minimal contig length reported in output (Default=200)

param.name: mincov
param.label: Minimum DNA %coverage
param.help: Minimum DNA %coverage to report partial allele at all (default 10, must be between 0-100)

param.name: minid
param.label: Minimum DNA %identity
param.help: Minimum DNA %identity of full allelle to consider 'similar' (default 95, must be between 0-100)

param.name: minscore
param.label: Minimum score to match scheme
param.help: Minumum score out of 100 to match a scheme

citations.citation: @misc{pope_dashnow_zobel_holt_raven_schultz_inouye_tomita_2014, title={skesa: eSKESA is a de-novo sequence read assembler for cultured single isolate genomes based on DeBruijn graphs. It uses conservative heuristics and is designed to create breaks at repeat regions in the genome. This leads to excellent sequence quality but not necessarily a large N50 statistic. It is a multi-threaded application that scales well with the number of processors. For different runs with the same inputs, including the order of reads, the order and orientation of contigs in the output is deterministic. }, url={https://github.com/ncbi/ngs-tools/tree/master/tools/skesa/}, author={National Center for Biotechnology Information }, }
